covered_report_window: Tuesday, June 16, 2026 after U.S. market close and Wednesday, June 17, 2026 before U.S. market open.
market_context_summary: The window is narrow and includes only three liquid, timing-confirmed U.S.-listed names: La-Z-Boy after the June 16 close, plus Jabil and CarMax before the June 17 open. Macro context is risk-on but fragile: U.S. equities recently rallied on U.S.-Iran peace-deal hopes and lower oil, while investors also face a two-day Fed meeting and residual uncertainty around energy flows and inflation.

top_setups.note: Only three liquid, timing-confirmed companies qualified in the immediate actionable window; only JBL screened as a positive consensus setup.

ranked_companies.timing: Before market open on Wednesday, June 17, 2026; call at 8:30 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.expected_driver: AI / Intelligent Infrastructure revenue and FY2026 guide quality
ranked_companies.key_risk_to_call: AI strength already priced in; needs beat-and-raise quality
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Jabil has the best upward setup because recent results and guidance showed broad upside led by Intelligent Infrastructure, but the stock already prices in a strong AI hardware narrative.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: MarketBeat shows Q3 FY2026 consensus EPS of $3.09 and revenue of $8.61 billion; Jabil guided Q3 revenue to $8.1-$8.9 billion and core EPS to $2.83-$3.23.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: AI-related infrastructure demand, margin expansion, and a clean FY2026 raise could overcome the high bar.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: A merely in-line quarter, weaker hyperscaler / AI commentary, margin pressure, or no incremental FY2026 raise could trigger sell-the-news action.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: OptionSlam showed weekly implied move near 9.85% and short interest around 2.39; Investing.com/Bloomberg cited an 8.9% expected move.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Recent preview coverage highlighted expected EPS growth and revenue growth, with analyst support tied to AI hardware exposure; the key issue is whether expectations have risen above sell-side numbers.

ranked_companies.timing: Before market open on Wednesday, June 17, 2026; call at 8:00 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.expected_driver: Retail used-unit comps, gross profit per unit, and CAF credit quality
ranked_companies.key_risk_to_call: High short interest and low-bar relief squeeze
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: CarMax leans down because the last quarter showed weak gross profit per unit, CAF headwinds, restructuring, and impairment pressure, while consensus still expects a sizable Q1 EPS decline from last year.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Benzinga cited consensus EPS of $0.94 versus $1.38 a year ago and revenue of $7.42 billion versus $7.55 billion; MarketBeat showed a similar $0.92 EPS and $7.41 billion revenue setup.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: A clean retail unit rebound, better gross profit per unit, lower credit-loss pressure, and management confidence under the new CEO could trigger a relief rally.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Another soft comp, lower retail GPU, higher provision / CAF pressure, or weak guidance could reinforce the April post-earnings selloff pattern.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: OptionSlam showed weekly implied move near 12.64% and short interest around 12.06; Investing.com/Bloomberg cited a 12% expected move.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Recent analyst notes remained mixed: JPMorgan stayed Underweight while raising its target, UBS initiated Neutral, and several firms raised targets after the prior report but mostly stayed neutral-to-cautious.

ranked_companies.timing: After market close on Tuesday, June 16, 2026; call Wednesday, June 17 at 8:30 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.expected_driver: Retail same-store written sales, margin, and FY2027 demand tone
ranked_companies.key_risk_to_call: Soft demand is known; relief possible on cash and share gains
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: La-Z-Boy is slightly downside-tilted but not enough for a clean bearish call because weak furniture demand and margin pressure are visible, while cash generation, no debt, and retail expansion leave room for relief.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: MarketBeat showed Q4 FY2026 consensus EPS of $0.82 and revenue of $569.23 million; Benzinga cited the same EPS and revenue consensus and noted EPS is expected below the year-ago $0.92.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Better written same-store sales, stable wholesale growth, cash-flow strength, and constructive FY2027 guidance could produce relief.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Weak traffic, Joybird softness, distribution/home-delivery investment drag, or cautious demand commentary could weigh after the print.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: OptionSlam showed weekly implied move near 11.65%, short interest around 3.86, and moderate event volatility.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Benzinga cited limited recent analyst action, including earlier upgrades from Sidoti and KeyBanc, but the current setup depends more on demand and margin commentary than ratings.

excluded_names.reason: Direction score above 30 but reversal risk above 40 because AI hardware strength appears partly priced in.
Absolute score near/above 30 but reversal risk is high due short interest and low-bar relief risk.
Absolute direction score below 30 and reversal risk above 40.

basket_distribution.why: No name met all allocation filters after consensus averaging.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
